title: Deterministic follow-up promotion policy
goal: Implement AF-03 from docs/roadmap/auto-frontier/04-promotion-policy.md. Add a pure deterministic policy module for classifying typed add_followup_slice drafts against a MissionEnvelope, slice/proposal graph view, and budget state. Keep naming obvious and explicit: prefer promotion_policy/classify_followup_proposal over names that imply a second workflow owner. No runtime wiring yet.
acceptance: A pure classifier returns TierDecision with stable reason codes for typed follow-up drafts using MissionEnvelope, graph view, and budget state inputs only.
The classifier has no filesystem, git, IPC, clock, worker, or daemon side effects and has no runtime callers in this slice.
Tests cover the AF-00 RFC scenario table, every reason code, Tier-3 precedence, exact area containment, budget/depth stops, and no-panic behavior.
Duplicate closed/open/pending/rejected/deferred cases classify according to the RFC, with rejected/deferred never auto-acceptable.
Documentation and names make clear this is policy/classification, not a second workflow engine.
must_ask_if: If the implementation would create a second proposal, decision, or apply authority channel outside the existing replan mechanism, stop and ask.
If required runtime behavior cannot be implemented within the declared areas, stop and ask rather than widening scope silently.
If a dependency or prerequisite from docs/roadmap/auto-frontier/00-matrix.md is not satisfied, stop and report the blocker.
If classifier behavior cannot be made deterministic without LLM/critic input, stop and ask.
